mission.title: nats: Performance degradation with slow consumers [v2.9.X, v2.10.X]
mission.description: Performance degradation with slow consumers [v2.9.X, v2.10.X]. This issue affects 4+ users.

steps.title: Identify nats analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your nats deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats -n nats --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review nats configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant nats configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl get configmap -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=nats
```
### Observed behavior

Performance degradation after the slow consumer connection. 
As you can see below, we are observing about 30% degradation of the incoming messages when the slow consumer connected, and about 50% after the second one.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Performance degradation with slow consumers [v2.9.X,
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
docker build -t test/nats:latest .
```

steps.title: Confirm Performance degradation with slow consumers is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats -n nats --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n nats --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the linked issue and PR for the community-verified solution.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with nats installed or the issue environment reproducible.
